selection_rule: Accept only if validation log loss is strictly lower than the accepted baseline.

candidates.name: Opponent-strength rolling average
candidates.hypothesis: A rolling win-rate estimate weighted by the quality of recently faced opponents may capture form more precisely than an unweighted recent summary and improve validation log loss.
candidates.planned_change: Add a train-only rolling feature that summarizes recent results against stronger and weaker opponents using historical rank-based weights. Reuse the current recent-window structure and keep the feature set incremental.

candidates.name: Rank-gap magnitude
candidates.hypothesis: The absolute size of the ranking gap may carry predictive signal beyond the signed difference, especially when mismatches are large.
candidates.planned_change: Add a numeric feature based on the absolute difference between p1_rank and p2_rank. Keep the existing rank features unchanged and evaluate only the extra magnitude signal.

candidates.name: Favorite underdog flag
candidates.hypothesis: A simple binary indicator for whether player_1 is the ranking favorite may help the model separate close matches from clear favorite situations.
candidates.planned_change: Add a float indicator for player_1 being better ranked than player_2, plus the complementary indicator if needed through existing features. Do not alter current rank features or target logic.

candidates.name: Smoothed recent match count
candidates.hypothesis: Recent-form estimates are more reliable when backed by more recent matches, so an explicit smoothed recent sample-size feature may improve calibration and lower validation log loss.
candidates.planned_change: Add recent match count features derived from the same train-only recent window already used for recent win rate. Apply simple smoothing so low-count players are not treated as equally reliable as active players.

candidates.name: Recent form volatility
candidates.hypothesis: Players with unstable recent results may be harder to predict, and a volatility feature from the recent window may help the model calibrate uncertainty.
candidates.planned_change: Add a train-only feature measuring variation in recent match outcomes over the current rolling window. Keep the implementation lightweight and based only on already available match results.

candidates.name: Overall recent blend
candidates.hypothesis: A blended estimate combining overall strength and recent form may outperform either signal alone by balancing stability and responsiveness.
candidates.planned_change: Add blended win-rate features that combine the current smoothed overall win rate and current smoothed recent win rate with a fixed weighting. Preserve the original component features for direct comparison.

candidates.name: Top-rank upset sensitivity
candidates.hypothesis: Matches involving top-ranked players may behave differently, and explicit upset-sensitivity features may help the model handle those edge cases better.
candidates.planned_change: Add simple indicators for top-ranked players facing materially lower-ranked opponents and for underdogs facing elite opponents. Keep the logic rule-based and limited to existing rank columns.

candidates.name: Recency-decayed history
candidates.hypothesis: Older historical matches may be less informative than newer ones, so a time-decayed version of historical strength features may reduce stale signal and improve validation log loss.
candidates.planned_change: Modify historical-stat feature construction to test a train-only recency decay on past matches while keeping the same core feature family. Apply the decay in a controlled way and compare directly against the accepted baseline.
